properties.remark: PENDLETON ASOS RECORDED A 60 MPH WIND GUST ASSOCIATED WITH A SQUALL LINE OF SHOWERS. SMALL HAIL THE SIZE OF A PEPPERCORN ALSO OCCURRED.
OBSERVER RELAYED A MESSAGE TO A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE ABOUT HAIL THE DIAMETER OF A QUARTER WAS ON GROUND.
GENERAL PUBLIC CALLED IN AND REPORTED HEAVY HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS.
GENERAL PUBLIC CALLED IN AND REPORTED HEAVY HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS. THIS OCCURRED IN UMATILLA COUNTY NOT WASCO COUNTY.

COUNTY DISPATCH FOR YAKIMA REPORTED LESS THAN 20 FT VISIBILITY IN THE TOWN OF HARRAH. A MULTIPLE CAR ACCIDENT OCCURED AS WELL. HARRAH ROAD WAS CLOSED DUE TO THE POOR VISIBILITY. POOR VISIBILITIES HAVE ALSO BEEN REPORTED IN THE TOWN OF SUNNYSIDE WITH A SEMI-TRUCK STUCK DUE TO THE BLOWING DUST.
BLOWING DUST HAS REDUCED VISIBILITIES DOWN TO ZERO AT THE INTERSECTION OF PUMP HOUSE AND ISLAND.
AMATEUR RADIO OPERATOR LOCATED AT PENDLETON EXPERIMENTAL STATION REPORTED A WALL OF DUST JUST NORTH OF THE STATION AND EAST OF HIGHWAY 11.
WALL OF DUST JUST WEST OF HIGHWAY 11 BETWEEN PENDLETON AND ADAMS WAS MOVING EAST.
VISIBILITY AT AHTANUM ROAD AND SLAVIN ROAD DOWN TO 100 METERS.
HIGHWAY 207 FROM MILE POST 20 TO 22 ZERO VISIBILITY DUE TO BLOWING DUST.
MESONET MEASURED 67 MPH WIND GUST AT GABEL MOUNTAIN.
DUST HAS BLOCKED ANY VIEW OF THE HORSE SEVEN HILLS AND RATTLE SNAKE HILLS. CLOUDS OF DUST ARE BLOWING DOWN TO STREET OF SUNNYSIDE.
VISIBILITY NEAR 100 FEET BETWEEN MILEPOST 190 AND 192 ON INTERSTATE 84. MOTORISTS HAVE PULLED OFF ON THE SIDE OF THE ROAD.
OREGON STATE POLICE WAS NOTIFIED OF VEHICLE PILEUPS ON INTERSTATE 84 CREATED BY DENSE BLOWING DUST. TWO SEPARATE PILEUPS WERE REPORTED NEAR MILEPOST 189. EACH PILEUP INVOLVED 3 VEHICLES.
*** 1 INJ *** MULTIPLE CRASHES IN THE AREA. VERY LIMITED VISIBILITY.
HAIL COMPLETELY COVERED GROUND WITH A MAXIMUM SIZE OF 1 INCH.

REPORT OF HEAVY RAIN AND UP TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL. THE HAIL FELL RANGING FROM NICKLE TO QUARTER SIZE FOR 10 MINUTES AND COATED THE GROUND. RAIN FROM THE HEAVY DOWN POUR COVERED THE BLACK TOP IN A MATTER OF MINUTES ALONG STRETCHES OF THE COUNTY ROAD.

HAIL HAS BEEN FALLING FOR 5 MINUTES WITH THE LARGEST MEASURED AT WALNUT SIZE. THE AVERAGE BEING BETWEEN NICKLE AND QUARTER SIZE. THE HAIL COMPLETELY COATED THE GROUND. WIND GUSTS OF 30 TO 40 MPH WINDS.
SHINGLES BLOWN OFF ROOF
60 MPH WINDS CAUSE A DUST STORM IN FRESHLY PLOWED FIELDS NORTH OF PENDLETON AND LIMITED THE VISIBILITY TO LESS THAN A MILE. THE WIND HAS STOPPED AND HEAVY RAIN IS FALLING CURRENTLY.
3480 FT ELEVATION
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN SR 125
4-6 INCH DIAMETER TREE LIMBS SNAPPED OFF TREE
ROUGHLY A HALF MILE NORTH OF THE BAKER COUNTY LINE IN UNION COUNTY. MOSTLY PENNY SIZE HAIL THAT COVERED THE GROUND WITH A DEPTH OF ONE INCH. LOST POWER.
WINDS BROKE THE TOP OFF A 10 YEAR OLD MAPLE TREE WHICH WAS 1.5 INCHES IN DIAMETER. VERY HEAVY RAIN, AND 3/4 INCH HAIL WAS REPORTED.
1 1/4 INCH HAIL REPORTED. 40-50 MPH WINDS. COULD SEE A WALL CLOUD 5 MILES SOUTH OF LA GRANDE.
DISPATCH REPORTED A TREE DOWN OVER HIGHWAY 3 AT MILEPOST 30.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL COVERED THE GROUND. HAIL CHIPPED PAINT ON THE CAR. HEAVY RAIN FOR FIVE MINUTES.
GOLFBALL SIZED HAIL FELL FOR FIVE MINUTES.
1.5 INCH IN DIAMETER HAIL REPORTED IN THE HIGH VALLEY AREA.
STRONG WINDS FROM A THUNDERSTORM BLEW DOWN A TREE WHICH TOOK OUT A POWER LINE CAUSING A POWER OUTAGE IN DAYVILLE AND A POWER OUTAGE ALONG HIGHWAY 26 FROM MILEPOST 140 TO MILEPOST 144.

MUDSLIDE ON US FOREST SERVICE ROAD 1308, INDIAN CREEK ROAD. EXTREMELY HEAVY RAIN SENT A LARGE AMOUNT OF MUD DOWN A STREAM AND SEPARATED TWO GROUPS OF CAMPERS. USFS CREWS SPENT ABOUT 5 HOURS CLEARING THE MUDSLIDE.
HAIL THE SIZE OF LARGE GUMBALLS.
HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF A QUARTER.
DEPARTMENT OF NATURAL RESOURCES FIRE CREW WORKING ON A FIRE 11 MILES SOUTH OF RIMROCK REPORTED ONE INCH DIAMETER HAIL AT THEIR LOCATION.
YAKIMA COUNTY SHERIFF DISPATCH REPORTED 1 INCH DIAMETER HAIL FALLING IN CITY OF YAKIMA.

THE OBSERVER SAID AT THEIR LOCATION THE RAIN WAS NOT HEAVY. AFTER TAKING A TRIP TO SURVEY THE AREA THE OBSERVER REPORTED THAT TO THE WEST AS FAR AS CHAPELLE ROAD,WATER WAS STILL OVER RUNNING THE BLACK TOP ROADS AND THAT AREAS OF MUD HAD WASHED ACROSS THE BLACKTOP. THEY ALSO REPORTED ORCHARD ROADS FLOODED AND FIELDS AS WELL.

FOLLOWING UP ON A HAIL AND HEAVY RAIN REPORT FROM 415 PM, A FARMER REPORTED THAT WATER WAS STANDING ON ORCHARD ROADS, AND THAT SOME OF THESE ROADS WERE COMPLETELY WASHED OUT. THE CANAL RUNNING PAST HIS FARM WAS RUNNING NEAR BREACH. THE FARMER INDICATED THAT IN A 20 MINUTE PERIOD HE ESTIMATED 2 TO 3 INCHES OR RAIN FELL.
GOLFBALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED BY TRAINED SPOTTER IN IMNAHA.
PING PONG BALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED BY THE PUBLIC.
HAIL ALMOST COVERING THE GROUND WITH SOME QUARTER SIZE HAIL. WIND FROM THUNDERSTORM BROKE BRANCHES FROM TREES WITH THE LARGEST 4 INCHES IN DIAMETER.
SPOTTER REPORTED MAINLY NICKEL SIZED HAIL WITH SOME LARGER HAIL THE SIZE OF A QUARTER.
TREE DOWN ON HOUSE. TIME ESTIMATED. APPROX. ELAVATION 2850 FEET.
